body:
## Purpose 

The purpose of this PR is to switch from using third-order upwinding in the Zalesak vertical transport Jacobian, to using first-order upwinding. This should help stabilize some of the Zalesak runs since first-order is more diffusive and it has also showed better results in terms of monotonicity (conservation of qt).

Closes #1671 

## Content
- A one-liner change on this [line](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/blob/21d123b649dbb63ad0fda9c6d3b0a318e5d7b226/src/tendencies/implicit/wfact.jl#L52) from
```
ᶠwinterp(ᶜJ, ᶜρ) * ᶠupwind3(C3(magnitude_plus_eps(ᶠu₃)), ᶜρc / ᶜρ) /
```
to
```
ᶠwinterp(ᶜJ, ᶜρ) * ᶠupwind1(C3(magnitude_plus_eps(ᶠu₃)), ᶜρc / ᶜρ) /
```
- Added regular CI pipeline job MBW, now with `dt=450secs`!
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.


- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Use first-order upwinding in Zalesak vertical transport jacobian
